mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to a general decline in economic activity and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth is above 3%, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which translates into reduced consumer spending and lower corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate a healthy and expanding consumer and investment demand, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST > 1.5M units annualized
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a rising Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals aggressive monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, which negatively impacts corporate profitability and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during high inflation regimes, or the negative correlation is not observed.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a strong and expanding economy, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of periods where GDPC1 growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant monthly increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust job creation and a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS increases by more than 150K in a month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong manufacturing activity and efficient supply-side capacity utilization, which underpins corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods where INDPRO growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and unemployment (UNRATE) is also low, it signals an overheating economy, increasing the likelihood of future aggressive monetary tightening, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS is low and UNRATE is low, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant.
